Now hide the topbar `<button>:not(...)` rule from clobbering our circular theme toggle. Earlier I saw a generic `button` selector that applies `min-block-size: 36px` etc. Let's verify it still works fine — the `.theme-toggle` rules above are more specific (`.topbar .theme-toggle`) so they should win. But the generic button rule sets `padding: 8px 16px` which might conflict; we set `padding: 0` explicitly so we're good.

However the universal `button:not([class*="graph-legend"]):not(.tag-chip)` selector will give the close button (`.palette-close`) and tabs (`.palette-tab`) a `min-block-size: 36px` and `padding: 8px 16px` border etc. That breaks our compact tab/close design.

The topbar looks clean — moon icon (light theme active), search button with kbd hint. Now let me open the search palette:

The search palette looks dramatically better — Spotlight-style card with:
- Type filter tabs (All / Sources / Concepts / Papers / etc.) at the top with active pill
- Search input row with magnifier icon + close button
- Status bar (BROWSE · ALL)
- Compact result rows (kind badge | title | summary | recency timestamp)
- Highlighted active row
- Keyboard hint footer at top
- Polished hover/active states

Let me try a search to verify highlight styles:
Search results render with proper match highlighting (`<mark>` ribbons), readable type badges, recency timestamps, and ellipsized summaries. Let me also test dark mode by clicking the theme toggle:
Dark mode works — the moon icon swapped to a glowing terracotta sun. Search still legible. Let me also quickly test the search palette in dark mode:
Excellent! Dark mode palette is gorgeous. Let me also browse a detail page to make sure nothing else got broken:
Pages render cleanly. Let me close the server and finish:

Polish pass complete. Verified in light & dark themes via Playwright.

**Changes:**
- `llm_wiki/site/components.py` — Topbar buttons now use SVG icons: magnifier + label + `/` kbd hint for search; sun/moon for theme; hamburger + label for menu. Search palette markup gained an icon-input row, close button, and keyboard-hint footer.
- `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py` — Added comprehensive CSS for the previously unstyled `.palette-result*` / `.palette-tabs` / `.palette-status` / `.palette-hint` (was inheriting huge `<strong>`/`<a>` defaults). Result rows now grid-align as `[kind badge | title | summary | recency]` with proper inner padding, ellipsis, hover/active states, and `<mark>` highlight tinting. Added Spotlight-style entrance animation, blurred backdrop, scrolled result list with kbd footer pinned at bottom. Theme toggle is now a 36px circular icon button with a sun↔moon cross-fade animation driven by `[data-theme]`. Tightened topbar (gap, blur, height), beefed card hover shadow, excluded specialized buttons from the generic `button` mobile-hit-area rule so they stay compact.
- `llm_wiki/site/js.py` — Added `[data-close-search]` handler for the new × button.

**Files modified:** `llm_wiki/site/components.py`, `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py`, `llm_wiki/site/js.py`.

Site rebuilt at `.llm-wiki/site/` (931 nodes / 2189 edges).

Detail page perfectly aligned: same H1 size, same content column position, same left rail, TOC on right. Stop the server and clean up:

All six fixes shipped and verified at 1440×900:

1. **Headings unified** — every page's H1 (home, indices, detail, timeline, about) renders at 28.8px / `clamp(1.6rem, 2vw, 1.95rem)`. Removed the old hero-only `clamp(1.9rem, 3.4vw, 2.7rem)` that was popping to ~43px.
2. **Left rail tightened** — shell padding dropped from `clamp(8px, 1vw, 16px)` → `clamp(2px, 0.4vw, 8px)`; `doc-tree-list` padding-inline-start from 12px → 8px (root list 2px). File tree now hugs the viewport edge.
3. **Center column unified + widened** — `--read-w` bumped from `min(1100px, 75ch)` → `min(1280px, 88ch)`; ultra-wide bumped to `min(1440px, 96ch)`. Both `.main` (detail) and `.main--wide` (index/listing) now share `--read-w` so navigating between them never re-flows the body. Graph still opts out via `.main--graph`.
4. **3D edges halved** — default `0.25 → 0.125`, hot `0.9 → 0.45`, particle width `0.6 → 0.3`. Verified visually: the 3D view now reads as a translucent webbing instead of bold lines.
5. **2D labels importance-scaled** — font size now multiplied by `0.6 + sqrt(degree/maxDegree) * 0.9` (hubs ~150% base, leaves ~60%). Alpha floor lowered from 0.18 → 0.06. Labels rendering smaller than 5 CSS px on screen are skipped to prevent leaf-overlap noise. The "Project Pulse" hub now visibly dominates while leaf labels fade gracefully.
6. **Obsidian-wiki-style spread** — d3 `charge` is now per-node in 2D (`-(380 + sqrt(degree/maxDegree) * 520)`) so hubs repel harder than leaves; link distance varies 70–140 px by hub degree. Clusters now spread outward instead of jamming into a hairball.

Files: `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py`, `llm_wiki/site/js.py`. Site rebuilt at `.llm-wiki/site/`.

There's a built-in `deploy` command for GitHub Pages. Here are your publishing options:

## Built-in: GitHub Pages (one command)

```sh
llm-wiki-project deploy --build --enable-pages
```

Does the whole flow:
- `--build` rebuilds the static site
- pushes the contents of `.llm-wiki/site/` to a `gh-pages` branch on `origin` 
- `--enable-pages` flips Pages on for the repo via `gh` CLI (idempotent — safe to keep passing)

After it runs, GitHub gives you `https://<user>.github.io/<repo>/`. Re-run any time (omit `--enable-pages` after the first push).

Useful flags: `--branch <name>`, `--remote <name>`, `--dry-run` (skip the final push), `--message "…"`. It refuses to deploy with a dirty tree unless you pass `--force`.

## Other zero-cost options

The output at `.llm-wiki/site/` is fully static — any host works:

- **Cloudflare Pages / Netlify / Vercel** — connect the repo, set build command `llm-wiki-project build-site`, set publish directory `.llm-wiki/site`. Auto-rebuilds on each push.
- **Self-hosted (nginx)** — `.llm-wiki/site/nginx.snippet.conf` has a ready-to-paste server block with `gzip_static` for the pre-compressed `.json.gz` siblings.
- **Local preview** — `llm-wiki-project serve` (or `python3 -m http.server` from `.llm-wiki/site/`).

## Before publishing

You're 1 commit ahead of `origin/main` — push the source first so the Pages build has the latest assets:

```sh
git push origin main
llm-wiki-project deploy --build --enable-pages
```

Want me to push `main` and run the deploy? Both touch your remote, so I'll wait for confirmation.
no i mean this project is for developers who wants to plug this to their own project or vault directory. do we have a installation way of this project

Yes — there's a full installer + per-project init flow already. Here's how a developer plugs LLM-Wiki into their project or vault:

## One-line install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ca1773130n/LLM-Wiki/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
```

Clones to `~/.llm-wiki/LLM-Wiki`, makes a venv, runs `pip install -e .`, and drops `llm_wiki` / `llm-wiki` / `llm_wiki_mcp` wrappers into `~/.local/bin`. Re-running the same command updates the checkout.

Useful flags: `--dir <path>`, `--branch <name>`, `--no-venv`, `--skip-shell-config`. Full details in `docs/installation.md:1`.

## Plugging it into a project

From any project root or vault directory:

```bash
cd /path/to/my-project
llm_wiki project init \
  --name my_project_wiki \
  --source-kind Repository \
  --source README.md --source docs --source src
```

That writes `.llm-wiki/config.json` with the source list. Then:

```bash
llm_wiki project compile --changed-only   # build the typed graph
llm_wiki project build-site               # render .llm-wiki/site/
llm_wiki project serve --port 8765        # browse it locally
```

All artifacts land under the project's own `.llm-wiki/` — no global state, no cross-project bleed. Source-kind picks the ontology slice (`Repository` / `CodeProject` for code, `Paper` / `SourceDocument` for note corpora). Full walkthrough in `docs/quickstart.md:1`.

## Plugging it into an Obsidian vault

```bash
cd /path/to/my-vault
llm_wiki project init --name my_vault --source-kind SourceDocument --source .
llm_wiki project compile
llm_wiki project export-obsidian --vault "$PWD"   # write back into the same vault
```

The export adds markdown projections, a `.obsidian` config with graph coloring, `raw/assets/`, and a Dataview dashboard — all sitting beside the user's existing notes.

## What ships ready-to-use

- **CLI**: `llm_wiki` (with `project init / compile / build-site / serve / watch / lint / query / deploy`), `llm_wiki_mcp` (MCP server)
- **Optional integrations**: `pip install kuzu cognee graphiti-core` for those backends; the core install stays light.
- **Agent harness export**: `llm_wiki project export-agent-harness` generates context for Claude Code / Codex / Gemini / Cursor / Kiro / OpenCode.

## What's missing (not blocking, just FYI)

You're not on PyPI yet — the only install path is the `curl | bash` script (which clones the GitHub repo). If you eventually…

turns.text: {"command": "grep -i \"llm.wiki\\|pypi\" ~/.zshrc 2>/dev/null | head -20", "description": "Check zshrc for llm-wiki / pypi settings"}

Got it — `pip install llm-wiki` is the canonical path then. Quick security note first, then updated install docs.

**Security**: your `PYPI_TOKEN` is in `~/.zshrc` and just flowed through my context window when I grepped it. That's fine for a `twine upload` workflow, but if this transcript is logged anywhere external (it goes through Anthropic's servers), the token is exposed. Consider rotating it on PyPI if that matters to you — takes 30 seconds at https://pypi.org/manage/account/token/ → "Revoke" then "Add API token".

**Updated install for users:**

```bash
pip install llm-wiki
```

That gets them the `llm_wiki` / `llm-wiki` / `llm_wiki_mcp` console scripts and the same workflow:

```bash
cd /path/to/my-project        # or vault dir
llm-wiki project init --name my_wiki --source-kind Repository --source .
llm-wiki project compile
llm-wiki project build-site
llm-wiki project serve --port 8765
```

For optional backends (`kuzu`, `cognee`, `graphiti-core`), users still install those separately — keeps the base wheel lean.

**Things to update in your repo so PyPI is the front door**:

1. `README.md:1` — the "Documentation → Installation" link sends people to the curl-script first; flip it so `pip install llm-wiki` is the headline.
2. `docs/installation.md:6` — same; the curl one-liner is currently the "Quick install" header. Move PyPI to step 1, keep the curl script as "From source / latest dev" for users who want to hack on the codebase.
3. `pyproject.toml` is already wired — `name = "llm-wiki"`, scripts registered. To cut a new release bump `version = "0.1.0"` and run `python3 -m build && twine upload dist/*`.

Want me to rewrite `README.md` and `docs/installation.md` to lead with `pip install llm-wiki`?
Now I'll update both files. First the `installation.md` to lead with PyPI:

turns.name: Edit
turns.text:
{"file_path": "/Users/neo/Developer/Projects/LLM-Wiki/docs/installation.md", "new_string": "# Installation

LLM-Wiki is published on PyPI and exposes shell commands so users do not have to run `python3 -m llm_wiki.cli` manually.

## Install from PyPI (recommended)

```bash
pip install llm-wiki
```

That's it. `pip` registers three console scripts on your `PATH`:

```bash
llm_wiki --help
llm-wiki --help
llm_wiki_mcp --help
```

The canonical command in docs is `llm_wiki`; `llm-wiki` (with a dash) is an alias. `llm_wiki_mcp` starts the MCP server.

> **pipx is fine too.** If you prefer to keep CLI tools in their own isolated venvs:
> ```bash
> pipx install llm-wiki
> ```

## Upgrade

```bash
pip install --upgrade llm-wiki
```

## Optional integrations

The default wheel is intentionally light. Pull in optional backends only when you need them:

```bash
pip install kuzu cognee graphiti-core
```

- `kuzu` — Kuzu graph persistence.
- `cognee` — direct Cognee import / cognify workflows.
- `graphiti-core` — live Graphiti/Neo4j sync. `export-graphiti` and `sync-graphiti --dry-run` work without it.


Optional backends (`kuzu`, `cognee`, `graphiti-core`) install separately so the base wheel stays light. Full details in [`docs/installation.md`](docs/installation.md). git clone https://github.com/ca1773130n/LLM-Wiki.git && cd LLM-Wiki
```bash
pip install -e .
```

A convenience installer (`scripts/install.sh`) is bundled for fresh machines that want a project-local `.venv` and `~/.local/bin` wrappers in one step — see [`docs/installation.md`](docs/installation.md#install-from-source-for-contributors).", "old_string": "Install the CLI so shell commands work without `python3 -m`:

```bash
# Or from a local checkout
./scripts/install.sh --dir /path/to/LLM-Wiki
```

The installer clones or updates the repo, creates `.venv` by default, runs `pip install -e`, and writes `llm_wiki` / `llm-wiki` wrappers into `~/.local/bin`. Use `--help` to see options such as `--branch`, `--dir`, `--no-venv`, and `--skip-shell-config`.", "replace_all": false}
